questions.question: Why is Linux knowledge foundational for many DevOps roles?
options: A) Because web browsers only run on Linux
B) Because servers, automation, containers, and operational tooling frequently rely on Linux concepts
C) Because Linux replaces version control
D) Because cloud platforms disallow other operating systems
questions.correctAnswer: B) Because servers, automation, containers, and operational tooling frequently rely on Linux concepts

questions.question: What is the operational value of scripting?
options: A) Making repetitive work consistent and automatable
B) Replacing infrastructure entirely
C) Removing the need for testing
D) Styling terminal output only
questions.correctAnswer: A) Making repetitive work consistent and automatable

questions.question: Why does a DevOps engineer need to understand networking basics?
options: A) Because deployments, service communication, DNS, ports, and load balancing depend on them
B) Only to write SQL
C) Because networking replaces logging
D) It is only useful for frontend work
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because deployments, service communication, DNS, ports, and load balancing depend on them

questions.question: What problem does Git solve in an infrastructure or application workflow?
options: A) It versions changes so teams can track, review, and recover work safely
B) It replaces cloud providers
C) It starts containers automatically without configuration
D) It is only a ticketing system
questions.correctAnswer: A) It versions changes so teams can track, review, and recover work safely

questions.question: What is CI mainly trying to improve?
options: A) Repeated manual deployments
B) Early validation of changes through automated builds and checks
C) CSS layout consistency
D) Database sharding only
questions.correctAnswer: B) Early validation of changes through automated builds and checks

questions.question: What is CD in a delivery pipeline?
options: A) A database engine
B) The practice of preparing or automating software changes toward reliable release
C) A terminal theme
D) A browser extension
questions.correctAnswer: B) The practice of preparing or automating software changes toward reliable release

questions.question: Why are containers useful in development and operations?
options: A) They package software and dependencies into more consistent execution environments
B) They remove the need for networking
C) They replace source control
D) They only work for static sites
questions.correctAnswer: A) They package software and dependencies into more consistent execution environments

questions.question: What is the role of a container registry?
options: A) Storing and distributing container images for deployment workflows
B) Rendering frontend components
C) Encrypting shell scripts by default
D) Managing DNS zones
questions.correctAnswer: A) Storing and distributing container images for deployment workflows

questions.question: What does infrastructure as code improve?
options: A) It makes infrastructure definitions declarative, repeatable, reviewable, and automatable
B) It removes the need for cloud access
C) It only helps with mobile apps
D) It disables rollback
questions.correctAnswer: A) It makes infrastructure definitions declarative, repeatable, reviewable, and automatable

questions.question: Why is IAM important in cloud operations?
options: A) It controls identities, permissions, and access boundaries for users and services
B) It styles API responses
C) It replaces observability
D) It is only for billing dashboards
questions.correctAnswer: A) It controls identities, permissions, and access boundaries for users and services

questions.question: What does Kubernetes help manage?
options: A) Orchestrating containerized workloads and services at scale
B) Writing backend business logic automatically
C) Replacing all monitoring tools
D) Editing Git history interactively
questions.correctAnswer: A) Orchestrating containerized workloads and services at scale

questions.question: Why are secrets managed separately from application code?
options: A) To reduce exposure of sensitive credentials and control access more safely
B) Because code cannot read environment variables
C) To increase merge conflicts
D) Because secrets are only needed in development
questions.correctAnswer: A) To reduce exposure of sensitive credentials and control access more safely

questions.question: What is the purpose of observability in a running system?
options: A) To understand system behavior through logs, metrics, traces, and alerts
B) To replace incident response
C) To remove the need for deployments
D) To edit infrastructure manually
questions.correctAnswer: A) To understand system behavior through logs, metrics, traces, and alerts

questions.question: Why is rollback planning important in deployment strategy?
options: A) Because not every release works as intended, and recovery speed matters in production
B) Because rollbacks delete source control
C) Because production should never change
D) It only matters for design systems
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because not every release works as intended, and recovery speed matters in production

questions.question: What is the practical value of monitoring alerts?
options: A) They can notify teams quickly when important thresholds or failure conditions are reached
B) They replace dashboards completely
C) They store container images
D) They only matter during local development
questions.correctAnswer: A) They can notify teams quickly when important thresholds or failure conditions are reached

questions.question: Why does environment parity matter between local, staging, and production?
options: A) It reduces surprises caused by inconsistent runtime behavior across environments
B) It ensures every machine has the same wallpaper
C) It removes the need for tests
D) It only applies to frontend CSS
questions.correctAnswer: A) It reduces surprises caused by inconsistent runtime behavior across environments

questions.question: What does capacity or scalability planning address?
options: A) How systems behave as traffic, storage, and compute needs grow
B) Only how to rename Git branches
C) Only SQL syntax
D) It is unrelated to production systems
questions.correctAnswer: A) How systems behave as traffic, storage, and compute needs grow

questions.question: Why are runbooks useful in operations?
options: A) They document repeatable response steps for common incidents and tasks
B) They replace logging and monitoring
C) They are only useful for interns
D) They deploy infrastructure automatically by themselves
questions.correctAnswer: A) They document repeatable response steps for common incidents and tasks

questions.question: What is a key reason to treat security as part of DevOps rather than a separate afterthought?
options: A) Because delivery speed without secure defaults creates operational and business risk
B) Because security only matters after release
C) Because secrets belong in Git history
D) Because containers are inherently secure
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because delivery speed without secure defaults creates operational and business risk

questions.question: What is the strongest mindset for learning DevOps effectively?
options: A) Tool memorization without system understanding
B) Building fundamentals, automating repeatable work, and operating systems with measurable feedback
C) Ignoring networking and Linux
D) Focusing only on dashboards
questions.correctAnswer: B) Building fundamentals, automating repeatable work, and operating systems with measurable feedback

questions.question: Which tool is used for containerization?
options: A) Git
B) Docker
C) Jenkins
D) Maven
questions.correctAnswer: B) Docker

questions.question: Which tool is used for CI/CD?
options: A) Docker
B) Jenkins
C) GitHub
D) Nginx
questions.correctAnswer: B) Jenkins
